did.unittitle: James Jesse Strang collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection that documents and explores the life of religious leader, politician and self-proclaimed monarch James Jesse Strang and the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
James J. Strang's "Letter of Appointment": Restricted material. A reference surrogate has been substituted inBox 1, folder 4. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: James Jesse Strang (1813-1856)
bioghist.p: James Jesse Strang was an American religious leader, politician and self-proclaimed monarch. In 1844 he claimed to be the appointed successor of Joseph Smith as leader of the Latter Day Saint movement. Strang founded what became known as the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites). In addition to his ecclesiastical calling, Strang served one full term and part of a second as a member of the Michigan House of Representatives, 1853-1856, assisting in the organization of Manitou County. He was also at various times an attorney, educator, temperance lecturer, newspaper editor, Baptist minister, correspondent for the New York Tribune , and amateur scientist. He died from bullet wounds he sustained from an assassination in July 1856.

bioghist.head: Charles Jesse Strang (1851-1916)
bioghist.p: Charles Jesse Strang was the son of James Jesse Strang and Elvira Eliza Field Strang Baker (1830-1910). He was the editor and publisher of the Charlevoix Journal in Charlevoix, Michigan, 1883-1890, as well as postmaster in Charlevoix in 1887. He wrote about his father in "Beaver Island and Its Mormon Kingdom" in J. C. Wright, The Ottawan: A Short History of the Villages and Resorts Surrounding Little Traverse Bay (Lansing, Michigan : R. Smith & Co., 1895), 63-68.

bioghist.head: Milo Milton Quaife (1880-1959)
bioghist.p: Milo Milton Quaife was a historian, archivist, editor, and author of Michigan and the Great Lakes region. Born in Nashua, Iowa, he received his education at Grinnell College and the University of Missouri and his doctorate from the University of Chicago in 1908. He taught at the Lewis Institute in Chicago, Wayne University and the University of Detroit. He served as superintendent of the State Historical Society of Wisconsin from 1914 until 1920, as well as Secretary and Editor of the Burton Historical Collection at the Detroit Public Library from 1924 until he retired in 1947.

Quaife was an editor of the Wisconsin Magazine of History from its founding in 1917 to 1922, as well as managing editor of Mississippi Valley Historical Review from 1924 to 1930, and editor the Lakeside Classics Series, an imprint of The Lakeside Press, from 1916 to 1959. Quaife also authored more than 100 works including his work about James Jesse Strang, Kingdom of St. James (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 1930)
Quaife died in an automobile accident on September 1, 1959.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The James Jesse Strang Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: James Jesse Strang and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) Collection series, gift of William Robertson Coe, 1951.
Charles Jesse Strang Papers, Milo Quiafe Papers, and Visual Resources series, gift of Charles Eberstadt, 1956.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Charles Jesse Strang, a son of James Jesse Strang, compiled most of the material in this collection, which after his death in 1916 passed to his brother Clement James Strang, who then gave the material to Milo Milton Quaife. Quaife drew on the material for his book, Kingdom of St. James (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 1930). Quaife sold the material, probably between 1948 and 1951, to the bookseller Edward Eberstadt & Sons, which hired Dale L. Morgan to describe it in an item-level catalog, The Strang Manuscripts , in 1951. The bookseller then sold the material to William Robertson Coe, who gave it to Yale University in 1951. Charles Eberstadt from the firm then gave the remaining material in this collection as a gift in 1956.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Jesse Strang Collection. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The James Jesse Strang Collection documents and explores the life of religious leader, politician and self-proclaimed monarch James Jesse Strang and the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

James Jesse Strang and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) Collection, 1832-1869, (Series I) represents the bulk of the material in the collection. It consists of more than 550 items that relate to the early life of Strang as well as his founding of what became known as the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) and through his death in 1856. It includes substantial examples of his correspondence as well as church documents. The correspondence includes the "letter of appointment," June 18, 1844, attributed to Joseph Smith, founder of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, which Strang and his followers believed designated him as the successor to Smith.
Charles Jesse Strang Papers, 1871-1898, (Series II) consists of material collected by Charles Jesse Strang about his father, James Jesse Strang and the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

Milo Milton Quiafe Papers, 1870-1947, (Series III) consists of material, 1870-1947, collected by Quaife for his book, Kingdom of St. James (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 1930). Significant portions of the early material in this series were likely initially collected by Charles Jesse Strang, circa 1870-1916, and augmented afterward by the collecting of his brother Clement James Strang.

Visual Resources, 1870-1943, (Series IV) consists of original photographs and prints as well as copy photographs of portraits, documents, and publications related to James Jesse Strang and the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. James Jesse Strang and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) Collection, 1832-1869. II. Charles Jesse Strang Papers, 1871-1898. III. Milo Milton Quiafe Papers, 1870-1947. IV. Visual Resources, 1870-1943.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material is open for research.

In 1993, library staff used optical character recognition to transcribe the item-level titles and numerical designations created in 1951 by Dale L. Morgan in his description of "The Strang Manuscripts" for the firm of Edward Eberstadt & Sons.

In 2019, the processing archivist reviewed, corrected, and expanded on this transcription to reflect current archival practice and added descriptions for the Charles Jesse Strang Papers, Milo Milton Quiafe Papers, and Visual Resources. Morgan's original typescript description accompanies the collection in Box 3.

did.unittitle: James Jesse Strang and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) Collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of material, 1832-1869, that documents the life of religious leader, politician and self-proclaimed monarch James Jesse Strang and the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites). Material related to the early life of Strang include a fragmentary diary and miscellany. Remaining material covers the period after Strang joined the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in February 1844 as well as his founding later that year of what became known as the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) and through his death in 1856. It includes substantial examples of his incoming correspondence and drafts of his outgoing correspondence as well as church documents including branch and conference reports. The incoming correspondence includes the "letter of appointment," June 18, 1844, attributed to Joseph Smith, founder of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, which Strang and his followers believed designated him as the successor to Smith.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into six subseries: Basic Early Documents; Personal Papers; Church Archives; General Correspondence; Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts ; and Additions.

did.unittitle: Basic Early Documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes material, 1832-1855, related to the early life of James Jesse Strang as well as the early history of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers assigned in Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts (1951).

did.unittitle: 3. James Jesse Strang notebook
4. Joseph Smith, attributed, "Letter of Appointment"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter ostensibly written by Joseph Smith to James Jesse Strang from Nauvoo, Illinois, and published by Strang in the Voree Herald , January 1846, as well as in his The Diamond: Being the Law of Prophetic Succession and a Defense of the Calling of James J. Strang as Successor to Joseph Smith (Voree, Wisconsin, 1848) and The Prophetic Controversy: A Letter from James Strang to Mrs. Corey (St. James, Michigan, 1854). Accompanied by an unsigned note from a child of Strang.
Photostat copy.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted material. A reference surrogate has been substituted in folder 4. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

Personal Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes personal papers of Strang, including draft letters, writings, and family correspondence, 1841-1865. The material provides documentation the relations between Strang and his wives and extended family as well as about the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers assigned in Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts (1951).

Church Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of material from the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites), 1843-1850. Includes papers related to the organizations, such as the "Order of Illuminati" secret society and the collectivist Associated Order of Enoch as well as church trials, including proceedings against William Smith. A document also provides a roster of church members that witnessed the coronation of Strang as the "King of Zion" at Saint James, Beaver Island, Michigan, on July 8, 1850.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers assigned in Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts (1951).

General Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of correspondence, 1841-1869, by prominent individuals related to the development of the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites). Correspondents includes President George J. Adams, John Cook Bennett, Lester Brooks, Dennis Chidester, Samuel Graham, James M. Grieg, Alden Hale, Peter Hess, Lorenzo Dow Hickey, Andrew G. Jackson, Amos Lowen, William Marks, Jesse Nichols, Isaac Paden, Ebenezer Page, Stephen Post, William Smith, and Gilbert Watson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers assigned in Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts (1951).

Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript volume housed in a spring-back binding that provides an item-level description of material in the James Jesse Strang and Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites) Collection created by Dale L. Morgan for bookseller Edward Eberstadt & Sons in 1951. The preface provides a summary as well as a lengthy description of the collection encompassed in Series I as well as a brief biographical sketch of James Jesse Strang.

did.unittitle: Dale L. Morgan, The Strang Manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries comprises original material created by or sent to James Jesse Strang that was identified by the processing archivist in 2019 while reviewing the research files that comprise the Charles Jesse Strang Papers and Milo Milton Quaife Papers series of the collection.

Milo Quiafe Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of material, 1870-1947, collected by Milo Milton Quaife for his book, Kingdom of St. James (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 1930). Significant portions of the early material in this series were likely initially collected by Charles Jesse Strang, circa 1870-1916, and augmented afterward by the collecting of his brother Clement James Strang.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into four subseries: Research Files, Interviews, Transcriptions, and Correspondence.

Visual Resources

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of visual resources, 1870-1943, related to James Jesse Strang and the Church of Jesus Christ (Strangites). It consists of original photographs and prints as well as copy photographs of portraits, documents, and publications. The material was collected by Milo Milton Quaife for his book, Kingdom of St. James (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 1930) and likely includes images initially collected by Charles Jesse Strang, circa 1870-1916, and augmented afterward by the collecting of his brother Clement James Strang.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into two subseries: Original Photographs and Prints, and Copy Photographs.
